title: Keisha P4 Science — Properties of Sound

questions.text: What is loudness?

options.B: How high or low a sound is
options.D: How loud or soft a sound is

questions.answer: How loud or soft a sound is

questions.text: What type of sound do doctors use to see inside the body?
Ultrasound images can show organs such as the ___liver___ and heart.
An object vibrating quickly produces a ___high___ pitch. (low or high)
Thunder is an example of:
The quick back and forth movement of an object is called ___vibration___.
What does our brain do with electrical signals from our ears?
When more ___energy___ is used to make a sound, it is louder.
Explain how we hear a sound from a drum being hit. Start from the drum being hit to the brain hearing the sound.
The drum skin moving up and down quickly is an example of:
When you ring a bell, it vibrates quickly and produces:
A ___shorter___ bar produces a higher pitch than a longer bar.
The ___thickness___ of a string also affects the pitch.
How does sound travel through air?
When vibrations reach our ears, they are changed into:
What do we use to hear sounds?
Doctors use ___ultrasound___ to see inside a patient's body.
What happens when you tap a drum skin?
Name 3 factors that affect the pitch of a sound produced by a string instrument. Explain how ONE of them works.
When a string is pulled tightly, it is more ___taut___ and produces a higher pitch.
Ultrasound has a very ___high___ pitch that human ears cannot hear.
A drum produces a low-pitched sound and a bell produces a high-pitched sound. Explain why they are different using the word 'vibration'.
Ultrasound can be used to see:

options.C: A baby inside a mother's womb

questions.answer: A baby inside a mother's womb

questions.text: The place where a sound is produced is called the ___source___ of sound.
What affects the loudness of a sound?

options.C: Amount of energy used to make it

questions.answer: Amount of energy used to make it

questions.text: What is a vibration?

options.C: A quick back and forth movement

questions.answer: A quick back and forth movement

questions.text: A longer string on a guitar produces what kind of pitch?
Materials and sizes of objects affect:

options.C: The pitch of the sound they produce

questions.answer: The pitch of the sound they produce

questions.text: If you tap an object gently, the sound is usually:
An object vibrating slowly produces a ___low___ pitch. (low or high)
A whisper is an example of:
Ultrasound has a pitch that is:

options.C: Too high for human ears to hear
options.D: Too low for human ears to hear

questions.answer: Too high for human ears to hear

questions.text: A short bar on a xylophone produces what kind of pitch?
A ___thinner___ string produces a higher pitch than a thicker string.
When less energy is used, the sound is ___softer___. (louder or softer)
When you blow harder into a whistle, the sound is:

The source of sound is the:

options.C: Place where a sound is produced

questions.answer: Place where a sound is produced

questions.text: Explain 2 factors that affect how loud or soft a sound is.
Sound travels through air as invisible ___waves___.
The ___length___ of a string affects the pitch of the sound it produces.

___Distance___ from the source of sound affects how loud or soft it sounds.
Sound is produced when an object ___vibrates___.
Why can we not hear ultrasound but machines can use it to take pictures inside our body?
When you hit a drum, it vibrates slowly and produces:
Sound waves travel away from the source in:
What determines the pitch of a sound?
A harp makes different pitches using:

options.B: Strings of different lengths and thickness

questions.answer: Strings of different lengths and thickness

questions.text: Which instrument uses bars of different lengths to make different pitches?

A ___longer___ air column produces a lower pitch than a shorter air column.
